title: Changes in factor demand and supply
description: Practice what you have learned about how changes in the supply of labor and the demand for labor affect wages and employment.
topic_path: Changes in factor demand and supply
Changes in factor demand and supply

assessment_items.question_content:
The market for hand carved sasquatch figurines is perfectly competitive. The labor market for the workers who carve the figurines is also perfectly competitive. Assume capital and labor are complementary in the production of wooden figurines.

**Which of the following increases the demand for labor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an increase in worker productivity
an increase in the wage rate
a decrease in the price of figurines
an increase in the price of capital
an increase in the wage rate for workers in other labor markets

assessment_items.question_content:
Kraken Catchers is a company that specializes in capturing and eradicating cryptids. It hires labor in a perfectly competitive labor market. 

**Which of the following would cause Kraken Catchers to employ more workers and pay a higher wage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is an industry-wide improvement in worker productivity.
The cost of a substitute factor of production decreases.
The price of cryptid capturing services decreases.
There is an increase in the minimum wage.
There is an increase in the supply of labor.

assessment_items.question_content:
**For a perfectly competitive market in which labor is hired to produce cell-phone cases, which of the following leads to an increase in the demand for that labor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an increase in the demand for cell-phone cases
a decrease in the price of a factor of production that can substitute for labor
an increase in the number of workers in this labor market
an increase in the minimum wage
a decrease in the productivity of workers

assessment_items.question_content:
Capital and labor are complementary in the production of chupacabra figurines. Assume factor markets are perfectly competitive. 

**Which of the following decreases the demand for labor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for chupacabra figurines decreases.
A decrease in the wage rate
Worker productivity increases
The cost of capital decreases
An increase in the wage rate for workers in other labor markets

assessment_items.question_content:
Shark repellent is exchanged in a perfectly competitive market, and firms in this market hire labor in a perfectly competitive labor market. 

**Which of the following would cause employment in this industry to decrease?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The price of shark repellant decreases.
The cost of a substitute in production increases.
Worker productivity increases.
Many unskilled workers enter the labor market.
There is an increase in the demand for labor.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eggs are a normal good sold in a perfectly competitive market that is currently in equilibrium. The market for the labor used in egg production is also perfectly competitive.

**What happens to employment and wages in the labor market if the income of egg buyers increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: wages increase; employment increases
no effect on wages; no effect on employment
wages decrease; employment decreases
wages decrease; employment increases
wages increase; employment decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
Technological progress has resulted in the change shown in this graph. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d2b9bf6ea413ad52bf1e33a7182095d.png)

**What happens to employment and wages in the market for labor based on the graph shown here?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: employment increases; wages increase
employment decreases; wages decrease
employment increases; wages decrease
employment doesn’t change; wages increase
employment decreases; wages don’t change

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph illustrates the market for labor that is used to produce board games.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following would cause the change shown here?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph of a standard labor supply and demand model. The horizontal axis represents the quantity of labor (labeled as Quantity of Labor), while the vertical axis indicates the wage levels (labeled as Wage). A supply curve labeled S 1 is a line that slopes upwards from left to right, starting from the vertical axis. Two demand curves, D 1 and D 2, are represented by lines that slope downward from left to right. D 2 is positioned below D 1. Two points, W 1 and W 2, are marked on the vertical axis. Two points, L 1 and L 2, are marked on the horizontal axis. Dashed horizontal lines from W 1 and W 2 join the supply curve S 1 and the respective demand curves D 1 and D 2. Similarly, dashed vertical lines extend downward from the intersection points of S 1 with D 1 and D 2 to mark L 1 and L 2 on the horizontal axis. The intersection points are the points where S 1 intersects with D 1 and D 2.

choices.content: The demand for board games decreases.
Worker productivity increases.
There is an increase in unskilled labor in this market
Large portions of the population retire
An increase in the preference for leisure

assessment_items.question_content:
Cheese and bread are complementary goods, and the price of cheese has increased. 

**What happens in the labor market for bread bakers as a result?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Employment and wages decrease.
Employment and wages increase.
The labor market for bread bakers is not affected.
The supply of labor increases.
The supply of labor decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following leads to an increase in wages and employment in the labor market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an increase in the demand for the good that labor produces
an increase in the supply of labor
a decrease in the supply of labor
a decrease in the tastes and preferences for the good that labor produces
an increase in the price of a good that is a complement to the good that labor produces

assessment_items.question_content:
The market for toothbrushes is perfectly competitive and the labor market for workers for this market is also perfectly competitive. Recently the wage rate for toothbrush workers decreased and the number of toothbrush workers employed increased.

**Which of the following is the most likely cause of the change in the wage rate and number of workers employed described?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the wage rate in another industry decreased
working conditions for toothbrush workers have worsened.
the price of toothbrushes increases
a large amount of labor exits the toothbrush labor market
worker productivity in the toothbrush market improves

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to employment and wages in Maxistan if the preference for leisure increases at the same time worker productivity decreases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the effect on wages is indeterminate; employment decreases
wages decrease; employment increases
wages increase; employment increases
wages decrease; employment decreases
wages decrease; the effect on unemployment is indeterminate

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is likely to increase the supply of labor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: immigration
increased preference for leisure
higher worker productivity
lower wages
the price of a good being produced decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
Workers in Burginville are equally productive at every age. The mandatory retirement age in Burginville is 45 years old. 

**Which of the following is likely to happen if the mandatory retirement age is lowered to 40?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of labor decreases.
The supply of labor increases.
Wages and employment increase.
Wages decrease and output increases.
Wages and employment decrease.
